what_it_does: Defines a React Architecture canvas that explains a universal Type Registry through sections for layers, slot composition, inheritance, types versus templates, registry consumers, type creation entry points, and cross-system embedding.
observations: Uses React hooks from the global React object with file-specific aliases.
Reads registry data from window.CV_REGISTRY and renders examples from registry queries.
Subscribes to registry changes and recomputes per-layer counts when updates occur.
Builds the page from small local presentation components wrapped in repeated Section blocks.
Uses several global UI/rendering helpers such as CanvasHeader, ViShape, TypeThumb, TypeCard, and TypeLayerBadge.
Contains mostly explanatory UI with some live registry-derived previews and counts.
Uses CSS class naming centered on cv-arch-* plus shared dsa button classes.
Includes a small formatter, describeAccepts, for slot acceptance metadata.
standouts: The file presents architecture concepts as an interactive documentation canvas rather than only static text.
Registry Types are shown through real TypeThumb and TypeCard previews where available.
The canvas connects explanatory sections to navigation and builder entry points through callbacks.
conventions: Functional React components are declared in one file and attached globally through window.Architecture.
Sections use a consistent title/subtitle wrapper before specialized content.
Optional globals are partly guarded with optional chaining and conditional rendering.
Inline styles appear for a few compact presentation details.
Registry concepts are represented with layer, family, tag, slot, default, template, and embedding language.
concerns: LayerLadder guards R?.LAYERS but then assumes R, R.LAYER_INFO, and R.query exist inside the map.
Several components depend on global symbols without local imports or prop injection.
Some explanatory copy is embedded directly in JSX, making content changes require code edits.
The Architecture effect uses an empty dependency array while reading a per-render R reference from window.CV_REGISTRY.
notes: This file is an in-app architecture explainer for the universal Type Registry and its surrounding composition model.
questions: Does R.subscribe always return a cleanup function when available?
Should the global registry and UI helpers be guaranteed before this canvas renders?
Are the hard-coded consumer rows and entry points intended to mirror live app configuration or remain authored documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file renders an architecture canvas explaining how the Universal Type Registry works and how surfaces consume it.
apparent_themes: Universal registry as shared composition substrate
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make the registry model understandable inside the product by pairing conceptual explanations with live examples from the current registry.
novelty: It treats an application architecture explanation as a navigable, registry-aware canvas with live thumbnails, lineage, and embedding examples.
contribution: This file contributes an interpretive documentation surface that connects registry data, visual type previews, navigation callbacks, and authored explanations of layers, slots, inheritance, templates, consumers, entry points, and embedding behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A React canvas that explains the ConceptV universal Type Registry with live registry-backed diagrams for layers, slots, inheritance, templates, consumers, creation paths, and cross-system embedding.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to help users and builders understand how composable Types move through the product and how new Types enter and participate in the registry.

symbols.does: Renders the full Architecture canvas and wires registry counts, navigation actions, and section composition.
symbols.description: This component reads window.CV_REGISTRY, subscribes to registry updates, computes per-layer counts, and lays out the architecture explanation. It passes navigation and builder callbacks into sections that can open the registry, templates, workshop, build canvas, or type builder.

symbols.does: Renders the introductory panel describing the registry as a shared shape for composable product objects.
symbols.description: SectionIntro presents the high-level story of converting hard-coded widget, wizard, and slide systems into Types at layers. Its copy frames Types as extendable, embeddable, queryable, and creatable through Vi.

symbols.does: Wraps a titled architecture section with optional subtitle and children.
symbols.description: Section is the repeated layout primitive for the canvas. It gives each topic a consistent header structure before rendering specialized section content.

symbols.does: Renders registry layers as clickable rungs with rank, swatch, description, example thumbnail, and count.
symbols.description: LayerLadder reads layer metadata and example Types from window.CV_REGISTRY, reverses the layer order, and shows each layer as a button. It combines static layer metadata with dynamic counts supplied by Architecture.

symbols.does: Shows a schema-style and visual example of slots for the system.widget.hybrid Type.
symbols.description: SlotExample fetches a specific registry Type, renders its slot definitions as JSON-like rows, and pairs that with a TypeThumb preview. It labels each slot with accepted layers, families, and tags, including optional and multiple badges.

symbols.does: Formats a slot accepts object into compact human-readable text.
symbols.description: describeAccepts collects accepted layers, families, and tags into a joined string. It returns "any" when no acceptance constraints are present.

symbols.does: Renders a parent doc.wizard Type and its descendants as an inheritance example.
symbols.description: LineageExample pulls doc.wizard and its descendants from the registry, displays them with TypeCard, and adds explanatory copy about inherited slots, defaults, and brand voice. It uses a root-to-children layout with a connector and caption.

symbols.does: Compares a doc-layer Type with a template-layer instance using explanatory columns and previews.
symbols.description: TypesVsTemplates fetches doc.widget, renders it as a schema-oriented Type preview, then renders a template-oriented version with a variables badge. It includes a navigation button for the templates canvas.

symbols.does: Renders a table of product surfaces and what each reads or consumes from the registry.
symbols.description: ConsumersMatrix uses a local rows array to describe Workshop landing, builders, slide library, templates, build canvas, and Vi chat. Rows with a cta render an Open button that calls onNav with the configured destination.

symbols.does: Renders four cards describing how new Types can be created.
symbols.description: EntryPoints lists in-context, standalone, and plan-level creation paths. Each card contains authored explanatory text and a button that either navigates to another canvas or opens the type builder callback.

symbols.does: Renders a registry-backed embedding chain from deck to slide to block to widget doc.
symbols.description: CrossEmbedDiagram fetches several Types from the registry and displays them as connected thumbnail cards. Its caption explains slot acceptance, cycle prevention, and cross-family embedding using the same protocol.
